entry.title: Re: New 2H 2023 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
Hi @Volker_Ruof 

Thank you for the valuable contributions you’re making to the Time Management solution.

And you need a reference date. This is necessary to define from which date the base unit shall start. Currently it is allowed to choose only the first of a month. So, you can´t create valulations that run from 15th january till 27th of September (and I never heard of such a requirement, drop me a note if you have got one).


The reference date and the base units might be enhanced in the future to enable different valuations, like for example "rolling 10 days", but for the beginning we start with the above mentioned options. But you see already that with this fields there is the theoretical possibility to become quite flexible !

We’ve received specific requests from customers about payroll processing scenarios where time valuation results need to span from the previous month's second half through the current month's first half. This requires greater flexibility in defining the reference date—ideally supporting mid-month or rolling valuation periods.

Given this requirement, we’d appreciate your insights on the tentative roadmap for enhancements to the reference date and base unit functionality. Is there a planned timeline or consideration for supporting more dynamic valuation periods in the near future?

Looking forward to your feedback.

Thanks,

Venugopal Chowdhary

entry.title: Re: Access https://sapes5.sapdevcenter.com/sap/opu/odata/iwbep/GWSAMPLE_BASIC/ doesn't work
entry.content: It has been retired: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/getting-server-reached-when-going-to-login-in-es5-trial-system/qaq-p/12808178

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
I fixed the issue by applying license after the installation. Below are the steps

  1. Install and run the docker image. Cannot login in GUI as default license is expired.
  2. Get the Hardware key from the terminal.
  3. Get the license from minisap using hardware key.
  4. Copy the license into the running container by command 
docker cp <local path the key file> a4h:/opt/sap/ASABAP_license
  •  Trigger license update. 
docker exec -it a4h /usr/local/bin/asabap_license_update​
  • Now login into GUI and check in SLICENSE t-code.
  • Now on every restart whenever the Hardware key changes. License should be valid.

entry.title: Re: Story Report Enablement
entry.content:
Hi Arren_C,

 

You can find all details in this IDP document:  https://d.dam.sap.com/a/JPG32pq/SAP%20SuccessFactors%20Stories%20in%20People%20Analytics-Enablement%20Configuraiton-V1.3.pdf?rc=10&inline=true

Kind regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: Custom Fields CPM
entry.content:
Hi

Unfortunately there is no standard enhancement available for CPM to add custom fields. Only option is to make changes to standard webdynpro app and store the details in a custom table. Enhancing the standard table will be a nightmare.

@Rahul_Jalan

entry.title: Re: 2023 Release Highlights in Seconds: SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition
entry.content: Great blog Nobby, we still miss you down under!

entry.title: Re: Posting rejected (UPDATE_REJECT).Order 104XXXX could...
entry.content:
Hello Jeremy_Deo,
Above mentioned points are possible cause for the Posting rejected  error.                                                                                                                                         
Thank You for reply

entry.title: Re: SAC ODE Story error
entry.content: I got this problem when entering 2025. It was connected to a table in the story. In the table, I had a filter on a certain time period passed time. When entering the new year of 2025, we did not longer load data for that period which I had filter on. Updated the time filter and it solved the problem. For the record, I'm still in "Enable Reporting Layout" which was suggested here earlier, but that still works to me. // Sebastian

entry.title: Re: Can we link in Reporting if the candidate is the same...
entry.content:
Hi Wanda3,

This is not standard. You would require to have the candidate ID or Application ID in the. Job Information table. I have seen this with some customers. The application ID or Candidate ID gets written from RCM to EC / Job Information when the person is hired. With reporting you can then always link it back to the Job Requisition, Application and Candidate.

Kind regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: Credit Limit based on sales area or credit segment in...
entry.content:
Dear @hasan_ahmed1,

We need to assign the credit control Area to the different company in the below path, 

Goto SPRO --> Enterprise structure --> Assignment --> Financial accounting --> Assigning Company code to credit control area.
The sales area configuration is not required for this as we are having different company codes in this case.

We need to have different credit control area and Credit segment for each company codes.
Hope this helps!!!

Best Regards,
Surya.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Aggregate Values o...
entry.content:
Hi @guenay

I am not clear about the "dynamic date" you mentioned. How can we know the dates "202501" to "202503"? Is it a fixed date or the previous 3 months from the current month(today: 16-Apr)? 

One more case is below,

202501–202503 → 202604

202502–202504 → 202605

...

202511–202601 → 202702

202512–202602 → 202703

 

Regards,

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps

entry.title: Re: COGS split into fixed and variable in Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi, We have a Labour activity and in cost component structure it is defined as fixed and variable. In product costing reports we are able to see fixed and variable cost separately but the issue is when we sell the material to customer we want to see the fixed and variable breakup in Market segment report (cost of goods sold split) . When we configure cost of goods sold split, is there an option to post fixed value and variable value to different GL accounts?

Request you to kindly create a blog on adding custom fields to Master Project, Risk Register, Issue Register, Change Request etc.

Thanks

Abdul
SAP Commercial Project Management

entry.title: Re: MDK - Obtain section name from context in QueryOption...
entry.content: Thanks for your reaction Bill. If opening a feature request is the way to go, then it appears that is what we should do. I do hope these proxy classes will be added in the future, this would facilitate certain developments to an immense extent.

entry.title: How to attach file to Q&A and Blog Posts in Community 2024 ?
entry.content:
Is it planned to allow attaching a file to questions, answers, blog posts and comments? Currently, only links are allowed (i.e. publish the file in an external Web site and insert its link).

For instance, let's say I want to attach some source code with 100 or more lines, it's not really relevant to include it right into the question, but to attach it as a text file.

Also, if I want to add 2 or more text files (e.g. code as Git), that would be nice to accept ZIP files too.

If the policy is to not allow attaching files, do SAP Community recommend one external Web site to store and persist them?

Thank you.

Sandra

PS: apologies if the question was already asked but I couldn't find it.

Good day!

The email was change to my personal email but the universal is still the S User. 

I wanted to change the UID from my S User to my P User. 

Thanks,

 Joerom Ramos

Best regards,

Eberechukwu Nwaobiora

entry.title: Re: Transport Model from Dev to Prod
entry.content:
Hi Jain,

Best practice here is to create a connection in Dev and then transport the connection to Prod. In Prod you adjust the credentials, environment, etc. to match you Prod data source. When you build a model based on the connection in Dev and transport the model to Prod it will use this connection because it has the same ID as an object in your tenants. You don't transport the connection.

Kind regards,
Martijn van Foeken | Interdobs

entry.title: Changing my UID
entry.content:
Hello Team, 

Good day! 

I wanted to change my UID from S User to my P User. 

Any steps how to do that?

Currently I was able to change universal email to my personal account but my UID is still my S User. 

Thanks,

 Joerom Ramos

entry.title: Re: Identity Directory API specification is broken
entry.content: Hi, I've had the same issue. After opening a ticket with SAP they comfirmed the open API spec file is getting evaluated as an invalid file due to the presence of references to external third party sites in it. They sent me a new file that is working for me now. Hopefully they will update the Business Accelerator Hub file soon. I am not able to upload the API spec file in this forum.

entry.title: Re: Email Notification for Completed Supplier Invoice App...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

Yes, MRM_COIV_WF_APPROVE is applicable when using the “Create Supplier Invoice” app and clicking “Save as Completed”

When you use the Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and:

  • You click the Save as Completed button,
  • AND you have a workflow active for "Parked as Complete" invoices (scenario WS00800303),

Then the invoice is considered complete and submitted for approval via the workflow.

Once the invoice is approved in that workflow, the MRM_COIV_WF_APPROVE email template is triggered.

 

I hope this will help answer your last question 😊.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Error FAA_POST214 when reversing Asset Document of Le...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

I haven't even paid attention to the fact that you already talked about the mentioned note. My mistake sorry.

I also find another topic about the last error message you get following the instruction of the note 2544536.

Can you check this link and see if it can help you out ?

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/closed-fiscal-year-does-not-agree-with-legacy-data-transfer-date/qaq-p/6528262

If it doesn't solve your issue, hopefully a colleague of mine will take the lead on this topic.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: System Shows Message "one or more down payment requests exist and can be assigned"
entry.content:
You are about to invoice an outbound delivery invoice request. After click the invoice button, system shows the new invoice and you observe the message at the bottom: For Outbound Delivery Invoice Request ** one or more down payment requests exist and can be assigned.

You want to know why system shows this message.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: System Shows Message "one or more down payment reques...
entry.content:
Dear requester,

In your system, there are one or some down payment requests open and could be applied into the invoice request, so system shows the alert. You can choose to add the down payment manually.

To verify, you can edit the outbound delivery invoice request , In the Assigned down payments, click Add Row, from the value help list, there are some down payment requests available.

Also, you can see the available down payment for the account by following steps:

Go to Customer Invoicing - Invoice Documents view, show 'Open Down Payment Requests', filter account=ABC, we can find the available down payment requests that available for this account.

 

Thanks,

Adie

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified

I would like to ask you for your help. I just passed the assessment needed to prolong the validity of my certificate, but the status of the validity of my certificate did not change. Is there any other action from my side needed?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Changing my UID
entry.content:
Based on the information provided, it appears that you would like to update the email associated with your SAP universal ID. If this is correct, you can follow the steps outlined below to make the change: 2962360 - How to change the primary e-mail address - SAP Universal ID (UID)

If you have any questions or further assistance, please feel free to contact us.

Best regards,
Judy

  1. create a keypair in keystore
  2. Download the signing request

     

How do we do the 3rd step , get signed certificate from CA ?

Any input on this will be helpful.

Raj Priya

entry.title: Re: Story reporting - Last Trans Seq
entry.content: Hi, Jeroen. Unfortunately, this option won’t work because the purpose of the graph is to display only promotions. If I enable the option Y in the Graph, it will look all events reasons from the job information, which would exclude relevant data. The Promotion event is not always the last event.

entry.title: Custom Accrual condition not being reversed
entry.content:
We are using standard sale rebate contract (0S01) and pricing procedure A10005. We added one custom condition as Accruals (because we did not want to settle this directly with customer) as ZREP which will be a percentage based condition of the business volume. During the partial settlement rebate condition RES1 is being corrected by RES2 which holds the previous settlement value but the custom accrual condition is not being corrected. It calculates the value based on complete business volume without reversing it. Below are few screenshots of config already done:

Any help will be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: is there is any table which provide Posting date of budget supplement document number.
entry.content: i have requirement of change in supplement or return in wbs element budget. i got the document number, but unable to find out the document posting date.

entry.title: Re: Story reporting - Last Trans Seq
entry.content:
Hi Mirjam,

Your question was about the Last Transaction Seq. In Story Reports this field is called Effective Latest Change and has exactly the same function as Last Transaction Seq in canvas Reporting.

In case you only want to capture Promotion Events then I suggest working with a Data Range filter and apply a filter on the Event = "Promotion". As an alternative you can create a change report where you focus for example on Pay Grade Change or an other field change that indicates that someone has been promoted. 

Kind regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: Question: Difference between Learning Proxy page vs L...
entry.content:
Hi,

The "official" info says that you can not proxy from BizX onto LMS. The My Learning tile is actually hidden when you are in proxy (even if you check "Learning" in the Proxy Management page. You should proxy from within the LMS admin to access the My Learning sections. 

The LMS proxy prevents from launching courses or exams in place of the learner. You can navigate and register, that's pretty much it.

You can however trick the BizX proxy, by creating a direct link in the LMS, add as BizX short cuts, and launch that shortcut while proxying. With this approach, you can do anything on behalf of the account holder. Note that the audit trails will not spot you are proxying, so just keep that in mind.

Hope this clarifies the magical mysteries of proxy.

Cheers,

 

Kris.

I wish I can open it in new window but didn't find a way to do that. such as window.open javascript. see my video please https://youtu.be/pdZRMit7ebQ I also have shared reports between windows and ASP.NET how to turn off hyperlink in WinForms (Windows) and allow it in ASP.NET Web?

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: This is my complete hyperlink formula it work perfect. "/Report.aspx?reportPath=D:\Business\Exa Cloud\Exa Cloud Source\ExaCloud\Reps\02\AccJV.rpt&reportSelection=" + URLEncode( "{AccTransHed.thd_brn} = " + ToText({VwAccTrans.tdt_brn}, 0, "") + " and {AccTransHed.thd_typ} = " + ToText({VwAccTrans.tdt_typ}, 0, "") + " and {AccTransHed.thd_num} = " + ToText({VwAccTrans.tdt_num}, 0, "") )

entry.title: Re: Problems with CAP App cds.persistence.skip
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: Sequential Multicast suppressing error details
entry.content:
Hi Ricardo,

Neither of these are possible, because OData is failing with a 400 error, so iFlow processing ceases at that point. Exception handling is invoked and within exception handling, we have the camel error available, not the OData error.

We dont have the option to interrogate the OData response via Groovy, before it enters Exception Handling. If we had that option, your suggestion would work perfectly.

Thank you,

Eoin

entry.title: Re: Email Notification for Completed Supplier Invoice App...

Regards,

Melle

entry.title: Re: successfactors api JobApplicationAssessmentOrder update

entry.title: Re: Exploring New SQL on Files Use Cases with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024 and QRC 01/2025
entry.content: @SeungjoonLee Is it also possible (or will it be possible) to share delta tables from the HANA data lake as a provider for other delta lakes recipients?

entry.title: Re: is there is any table which provide Posting date of b...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA (including Public Cloud), for WBS element budget supplements or returns, you can track posting dates of budget documents using the following tables and approaches:

Primary Table: BPGE – Budget Line Items (CO)

  • Table Name: BPGE
  • Purpose: Stores budget line items including original budget, supplements, returns, transfers, etc.
  • Key Fields:
    • BELNR: Budget document number
    • OBJNR: Object number (related to WBS element)
    • GJAHR: Fiscal year
    • BDGT_TYPE: Budget type (e.g., supplement, return)
    • BUDAT: Posting date of the budget document

Use this table to retrieve the posting date (BUDAT) for your budget supplement document number

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Enforce a secure re-authentication for each new SAP GUI connection, with SAP Secure Login Servic
entry.content:
Hi @Christian_Cohrs 

We have a need to use the option to force people to authenticate without SSO using Azure MFA.

What I've noticed is that even with sncForceLogin set to '3' and ServerSessionKeyMode to '1' you are not asked for Azure MFA per SNC connection if your Windows PC already has an organisation login with the same user elsewhere e.g. OneDrive. The connection does authenticate to Azure AD for each SNC connection but there is no prompt for MFA.

Is there some way to not only disable SSO but to also force MFA (with re-authentication) for each new SNC connection made to a SAP system?

Thanks & Regards, Nelis

entry.title: Re: The SAP BW Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content: Hi Christof! Thanks for your blog. I guess this applies for SAP Databricks. On the presentation deck it is written down that there is a partner connect to an enterprise Databricks. Could you please explain if the partner connect will provide the same Delta Share from the Object Store?

entry.title: Re: Catalogue activation pre requisits and ANID
entry.content:
Hi @Arvand  since you are asking about external catalogs, yes the trading relationship between the buyer and the supplier on SAP Business Network is a prerequisite. Without it, the supplier will not be able to upload the catalog for the buyer and therefore the buyer will not receive the catalog.

Also, the supplier record must be maintained in the core administration of the procurement application. It can be done in different ways, but it is recommended that you always use the same method of
adding suppliers to ensure that supplier data is properly synchronized. You can maintain the supplier records:

1. By pulling the supplier data from an ERP

2. or from the supplier’s profile on SAP Business

3. or you can add the supplier manually using the Supplier Manager workspace. If you choose this method,
you create the common supplier first and then add the ANID to it.

Best regards

Marco

entry.title: Re: Anyone taken the sap C_S4CFI_2504 exam recently? Need...
entry.content:
Hi john, I did the C_S4CFI_2504 last week. Yes, the Learning Journey helps, but I felt the same, the org structure stuff in cloud can be weird at first. Took me a bit to really get how company code, chart of accounts, and business partner roles connect, especially with CBC in the mix.

A few areas I’d say are worth extra time:

  • Fiori apps — not just what they’re called, but what they actually do
  • Scope items + fit-to-standard process
  • Finance flows like AP/AR, asset accounting, and period-end
  • Basic integration concepts (APIs, comm arrangements, etc.)

Some of the trickier stuff was around extensibility (what’s in-app vs side-by-side) and also some questions on test automation and release cycles that felt a little out of left field.

As for practice, I used CertBoosters, not perfect, but honestly way better than the tiny set of sample questions SAP gives you and most of the random stuff out there. The practice test questions felt similar in style to the real thing and helped me figure out what I didn’t fully understand yet.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: is there is any table which provide Posting date of b...
entry.content: Hi, BUDAT Is not exist in table

entry.title: Outgoing Payment Print Layout Design has limitation on its Repetitive row details
entry.content:
Hi All

Please help confirm SAP Business One Outgoing Payment Print Layout Design has limitation on its Repetitive row details?  It does not print anything no matter what Field is placed on its Repetitive row details?

We managed to place Account Type Outgoing Payment GL Account Code on the Start of Report Header level but it will only show the first GL Account if there are multiple GL Accounts in Account Type Outgoing Payment.

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: View not updated in Front End - FIORI App
entry.content:
Hello Experts

We imported Fiori App changes to Production and strangely view XML not get updated in Front End. Could see the changes updated in SAP Front End Server. Strange part is Controller changes are updated in Front End. So remove caching also not yielding a result.

Any ideas?

View.xml => Not updated in Web => Could see changes in SAP Gateway Server

Controller.js => Latest updates visible.

Jeremy

entry.title: Unlocking the Potential of SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath
entry.content:
The rise of artificial intelligence (AI) and robotic process automation (RPA) technologies have empowered companies to leave behind manual, error-prone, and time-consuming work for streamlined operations, customized processes, and end-to-end automated solutions. The most competitive enterprises today are developing a lasting edge by taking advantage of these capabilities.

To give our customers an even greater edge with their automation toolbox, in Q4 2024 we released SAP’s Solution Extension with UiPath to integrate with their leading RPA to automate non-SAP environments.

With the new SAP Build Process Automations integrated with UiPath, customers can extend the entire SAP enterprise automation framework, whether they be between SAP and non-SAP applications, on-premises and multi-cloud environments, and systems that span customers, employees and partners.

 

How UiPath extends the value of enterprise automation with SAP
Enterprise automation from SAP uses best-in-class SAP technologies – SAP Integration Suite, SAP Signavio solutions, AI Agents and now SAP Build integrated with UiPath – to help connect and automate complex, end-to-end business processes. These technologies work in concert to enable customers to quickly and easily connect any on-premises or cloud IT landscape, including SAP and non-SAP systems, to gain visibility into business processes and their performance, and then automate those processes seamlessly.

With this partnership, it provides SAP customers flexibility to maintain their UiPath investments while leveraging a single process orchestration layer for both SAP Build Process Automation and UiPath robots to automate non-SAP processes.  As for automating SAP processes, we continue to invest in SAP Build Process Automation and its extensive pre-build workflows and automations for SAP applications. It also serves as the foundation for Joule Studio in SAP Build that customers can use to build AI agents.

Fast track cloud migration with proven accelerators
The move from customized ERP systems to S/4HANA Cloud is time consuming and risky while ensuring data integrity during the complex migration process.  By leveraging SAP Activate methodology and SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath robots with proven migration content, data is accurately extracted by identifying complex code, analyze vulnerabilities, check for compatibility with S/4 and remediate any issues automatically to simplify migration efforts and prevents manual errors.

Continuous process improvement to surpass competition
Organizations face continuous changes in processes, applications and industry best practices, which can break automated strategic end-to-end business processes.  To maintain process transparency, enterprise automation with SAP portfolio includes integrated triggers that recognize inefficiencies or events, and to adjust the predetermined behavior of automation methodologies for greater productivity in S/4HANA.  To minimize disruption from application changes, SAP iPaaS and RPA can automate core processes and utilize automation techniques.  As for best practices to remain competitive, SAP is the process leader across 25 industries where knowledge is captured in industry proven benchmark and pre-built content delivered through SAP Business Accelerator hub that can be rapidly deployed.


Automate enterprise processes to optimize your business
Automating strategic end-to-end processes often require a variety of automation technologies (RPA, workflow, systems integration, APIs and events powered by AI) across heterogenous landscapes.  Enterprise automation with SAP portfolio is a Gartner market leader with profiling, iPaaS, and now RPA, which provides the process orchestration layer for comprehensive interoperability across all these automation methods.

SAP and UiPath Together: One Platform, Limitless Potential
By integrating SAP Build Process Automation workflows with UiPath’s RPA and third-party connectors, we’re offering our customers one of the largest libraries of pre-built content and accelerators to help address their comprehensive processes that span multiple applications within a complex IT landscape—regardless of the cloud software provider.  To illustrate the power of SAP and UiPath synergy, let’s examine a business scenario that shows real life automations across SAP and non-SAP environments as well as other highlights across the integrated solution.

Procurement: MRP-to-goods receipt journey
Manual interventions in the source-to-pay journey can weigh down business operations.  When processes traverse many different applications, tracking down the process exceptions or even the status of any process instance can be a laborious task requiring highly specialized skills.  Let’s take a look as to how SAP and UiPath can work together to deliver a seamless procurement journey.

The process starts with an MRP run in a manufacturing operations management solution, like Siemens Opcenter

  1. The creation of the purchase requisitions trigger the SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath to create these requisitions in SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  2. Once in Ariba, the created requisitions are routed to the correct approver based on the SAP Build Process Automation business rules engine
  3. Approved requisitions are converted to purchase orders in Ariba and Oracle WMS is updated with the PO data for goods receipt
  4. Finally, SAP S/4HANA Cloud is updated with all relevant data to ensure prompt and accurate invoice processing.

As you can see, this process is relatively complex, yet the power of SAP and UiPath enable seamless automation across the many systems as well as a central monitoring view to be able to quickly respond to any processing exceptions.

 

Process improvement
To gain a comprehensive view of your processes across your ERP landscape, you can pinpoint bottlenecks and uncover areas with the greatest automation potential. More importantly, with contextual best practices and prebuilt content for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and other cloud solutions from SAP, your organization can receive actionable recommendations to refine its processes. For example, inefficiencies identified in SAP Signavio solutions can trigger smart actions in SAP Build—turning insights into impactful automations.

 

Enterprise-grade governance
Combine SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath’s capabilities and the comprehensive security features of SAP Business Technology Platform to help ensure compliance and trust in enterprise-wide automation efforts. 

 

Orchestrated task automation
Coordinate workflows with smooth connectivity to the SAP Task Center service, driving consistent and optimized operations.


Combined development tools
SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath combines workflow management, robotic process automation (RPA), and embedded AI into a unified low-code/no-code environment. Designed for business users and IT teams, the Automation designs originate as projects within SAP Build Process Automation, serving as the foundation for streamlined workflows and advanced process orchestration.

Key features include:

  • Integrated development tools to build business rules, approval workflows, RPA bots, and generative AI models
  • Comprehensive workflow management to orchestrate processes, monitor tasks in real time, and gain actionable insights.  Customizable process extensions help keep the ERP core clean while extending functionality with drag-and-drop tools and embedded security guardrails
  • Native integrations with SAP Integration Suite, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Signavio solutions, and other SAP software for a unified experience
  • Cost efficiency through native embedding
    Decrease integration and maintenance costs by embedding UiPath RPA directly into SAP Build Process Automation. Unlike standalone RPA solutions, SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath eliminate the need for extensive third-party integrations.
  • Advanced monitoring for real-time insights
    This integration features authentication enabled by SAP Cloud Identity Services that gives access to UiPath services, while lifecycle synchronization helps ensure automations remain aligned across both solutions. UiPath monitoring is also fully integrated into SAP Build Process Automation, providing unified tracking and enhanced visibility into automation processes.

 

  • Digital access license is not required to connect with SAP systems
  • Streamlined design and deployment
    Use drag-and-drop tools to efficiently build and deploy automation workflows. This capability simplifies the design process and accelerates automation implementation.  For example, workflows and processes can be streamlined by leverage bots directly within SAP Build Process Automation.

    •  

Advanced automation tools

  • Comprehensive automation at scale beyond SAP solutions: Launch automation projects with thousands of prebuilt templates for third-party systems to create a unified enterprise automation ecosystem across the business.
  • AI-driven automation for smarter workflows:  Leverage built-in AI capabilities, such as intelligent document processing and predictive analytics, to enhance the efficiency of automations. Unlike many standalone RPA solutions, the SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath combines AI and automation to handle complex, unstructured data and document processing into workflows.
  • Unified automation for complex processes:  Empower your business by automating entire workflows, helping ensure comprehensive process optimization across multiple systems and reducing fragmentation in automation efforts.
  • Cloud-based bot execution:  Transition to the cloud with bots to connect with SAP solutions securely and efficiently through remote calls and surface automation using screen recordings and UI-element captures.  This connectivity supports a range of SAP user interface technologies, including SAP Fiori apps, SAPUI5, GUI, WebGUI, and line-of-business software, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors solutions, and third-party applications. With SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath, these options provide prebuilt content, native connectors, and superior surface automation for a seamless integration experience.
  • Process optimization with advanced insights:  Leverage task mining and live insights into SAP Signavio solutions, so you can uncover inefficiencies and transform processes with actionable data.

 

How UiPath strengthens Enterprise Automation with SAP
With more than 50 years of expertise in mission-critical business processes, SAP continues to lead the way in enterprise automation.  By combining trusted solutions with the latest technologies, SAP empowers organizations to simplify operations, drive innovation, and scale transformation.

SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath are part of that long-standing legacy. By offering a clear path to unify, optimize, and future-proof your processes, the integration not only elevate automation strategies but also unlock new levels of resilience and growth.

Ready to see how enterprise automation can transform your business? Interact with our demo of SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Workarea Migration / Activation of Redesign...
entry.content:
Hi David,

regarding the ressources: The marked ressource has a red dot. That means it's imported from a connected workarea. Did you take a look at different ressources in your workarea (such as trainerstyle or the trainer itself)?

Regarding Companion: Did you configure the layout in your SAP-System with some parameters? Or did you modify anything in the Producer?

Unfortunately, that's not clear from the posting.

Best regards,

Peter

entry.title: Re: is there is any table which provide Posting date of b...
entry.content: Hi sir, i am am working on HANA RISE

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hi @poojarychetan 

If you follow my guide, your application job will always have 1 step. You can see the same in the Fiori app Maintain Application Job Templates (F2058). Here my example:

You see steps is 1. If you want to maintain several steps, you need to use this app, to create a new Application Job Template, where you now get the "Maintain Steps" button (at the bottom):

Once you click it, you can add as many steps (which in turn are again Application Job Templates) as you like:

So if you need several steps. First create them one by one as Application Job Template, then create a new "main" template which you then maintain the others as steps, and finally you schedule the "main" one to run in background.

entry.title: Data Migration material master data in japanese test environment error
entry.content:
we want migration the material master data to public cloud , but we found almost same data , when we use the english format the migration will be success , but when we use the japanese format the migration will be failed .

--- Steps to Reproduce ---

1 login  (Migration Projects ) 移行コックピット  and change the setting inb japanese .

2 create and select Migrate Data Using Staging Tables.

3 enter the project name : xxxx-test .

4 click step 2 , and enter the key word product , if use the japanese , pls use the key word as '製品'。 and select product .

5 and move product to Selected Migration Objects and click review .

6 select do not add .and create project .

7 back to the the Migration Projects  first page , log in the project : xxxx-test .

8 download the format as XML file .

9 the in put the data on the japanese format and english format.

10 the upload the format .

11 at this time we found at english environment we migration the data was success , but at japanese environment was failed .

Then I rebuild my different levels of hierarchies in my program?

entry.title: New Basic Trial available: Get your hands on Enterprise Automation with SAP
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business landscape, automation has emerged as a crucial competitive differentiator. While most stakeholders understand its strategic value and potential benefits, many organizations still face significant challenges on the path to becoming truly autonomous.

Enterprise Automation with SAP is the only solution that offers a comprehensive, integrated suite of tools for process mining, automation, integration, monitoring, and continuous optimization of workflows. It empowers businesses to streamline operations and drive intelligent decision-making—without adding unnecessary complexity.

 

 

With SAP Signavio, SAP Build, and SAP Integration Suite, customers gain access to best-in-class capabilities. These tools work in harmony, enabling users to quickly uncover insights, automate processes, and integrate seamlessly with existing applications to deliver the right outcomes.

Now, there’s a powerful new way to explore the full potential of Enterprise Automation with SAP. The new Basic Trial gives you hands-on access to SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio—all in one end-to-end scenario. It’s the perfect opportunity to experience the value of automation in action. In order to activate your basic trial, you need to navigate to the Enterprise Automation webpage on SAP.com:

Access to Basic Trial for Enterprise Automation

As the next step you need to click on the ,,Start your free 30-day trial" button that will forward you to the next page.

 

 

You are now being forwarded to the Basic Trial page of SAP Build Process Automation, here you simply click on ,,Try now". This process will generate log-in credentials which you can use right away to start your hands-on tutorial.

 

 

After logging in you will be redirected to the tutorial landing page. As a last step you need to select the Enterprise Automation workbook and start your tutorial:

 

 

This includes a 2 hour hands-on exercise with 5 different units, which you can discover at your own pace:

 

 

If you want to learn more about the Basic Trial and the use case, that is being covered in this hands-on feel free to listen to our webcast, which you can find here.

 

Happy hacking!

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud version 2 - Client Certificate authentication (Outbound)
entry.content:
Hello @RajPriya_05 , SAP is not a ceritifcate signing authority.

You have to get it signed by a Certificate signing authority. It is clearly mentioned at the start of the blog.

Regards, Abinash

entry.title: Re: is there is any table which provide Posting date of b...
entry.content:
Dear user,

Thank you for your reply.

For your previous question about finding the posting date (BUDAT) of a budget supplement or return document for WBS elements :

In RISE (Private Edition), the standard tables used in Investment Management (IM) and Project System (PS) for budget documents include :

1. BPJA / BPGE / BPBK

These tables store budget data, but do not hold the posting date (BUDAT).

2. FMIFIIT (if Funds Management is active)

Contains financial data but usually not relevant for just PS budget supplements.

3. Table IMAVC_OBJECT or FMBDT

Still might not give you BUDAT.

 

Best Option: Use Function Module or FM View

The posting date for budget supplements or returns is usually found only at runtime in logs or via function modules, such as:

  • Function Module: BP_DOCUMENT_READ
  • This reads the full budget document — including supplement/return information and posting date.
  • You can test it via SE37 using your document number.

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: s_alr_87013611 header is not displayed
entry.content: Can you tell us the hint to solve the issue?

entry.title: Journey into the Composable Storefront: Empowering Your Learning Path with Engaging Video Tutorials
entry.content:
Welcome to the video journey through the SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront! If you're looking to elevate your understanding of SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront, you're in for a treat. We've curated a series of insightful video tutorials designed to guide you on a fascinating journey through this innovative platform. Whether you're a beginner taking your first steps or an advanced user delving deep into complex functionalities, these videos promise to empower and enrich your learning experience.

Embarking on Your Learning Adventure: The Beginner's Path

Before diving in, let's set the scene. The Composable Storefront is a forward-thinking, lean, Angular-based JavaScript storefront specifically crafted for SAP Commerce Cloud. It communicates exclusively through the Commerce REST API and stands proudly as an open-source project. With resources available on GitHub, learners are invited to explore the depths of its source code—a true adventurer's playground.

Our journey begins at the beginner's level. If you're new to Composable Storefront, the introductory video, Introducing SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront, is your perfect starting point. Here, you'll familiarize yourself with the platform's capabilities and lay the foundation for your learning voyage.

Next, you'll venture into creating a local environment—imperative for hands-on practice—by watching Setting Up a Local SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront Instance. Arm yourself with these essential tools as you begin to sculpt your understanding.

As you proceed, Styling with Global Variables in SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront introduces you to the art of customization using CSS techniques. Learn to express your individuality and adapt styles to suit your unique project needs.

To enrich your beginner's journey, explore Configuring Infinite Scroll in SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront. Here, you'll harness infinite scroll functionality, opening doors to fluid, uninterrupted navigation tailored to your business demands.

With fingers on the pulse of customization, Extending the SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront with Outlets provides insights into UI tailoring, enabling users to modify their storefront with precision.

Finally, round off your foundational knowledge by mastering Connecting SmartEdit to SAP Commerce Cloud, Composable Storefront, integrating powerful web content editing capabilities with your store interface.

Venturing into Intermediate Terrain: Building on a Solid Foundation

With the basics secured, it's time to scale the heights of intermediate knowledge. Your path continues towards deeper technical insights, which will prove invaluable in complex scenarios.

Begin with Updating Angular Schematics in SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront—a powerful tutorial revealing how Angular Schematics can be updated to enhance your project’s efficiency and adaptability.

Then, traverse through Working with Custom URL Matchers in SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront. Discover how these matchers facilitate unique URL handling and their importance in crafting a seamless browsing experience.

The journey doesn't stop there; venture into Supporting Search Engine Optimization in Composable Storefront of SAP Commerce Cloud and unlock the SEO secrets critical to boosting your storefront’s visibility and reach.

Finally, enrich your understanding with Using CMS Data in SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront, a tutorial dedicated to integrating and managing CMS data, enhancing your storefront’s content versatility.

Scaling Advanced Peaks: Master the Art of SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront

As you approach the pinnacle of your journey, prepare to delve into sophisticated technology realms, strengthening your mastery over Composable Storefront’s potent features.

Your advanced quest begins with Introducing Server Side Rendering (SSR) in SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront, where you'll learn why SSR is crucial and how it operates, transforming your site’s performance and reach.

Next, dive into the depths of Understanding the Event System in SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront. This tutorial sheds light on event types and capabilities, an essential understanding for crafting dynamic interactions.

Lastly, encounter Working with Runtime Configuration Initializers in SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront, granting you the ability to expertly manage runtime configurations and optimize your storefront.

Concluding Your Learning Video Journey: A Summary

Throughout this engaging journey, you've navigated through beginner insights, intermediate complexities, and advanced intricacies of SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront. Each video tutorial serves as a vital checkpoint, honing your skills and deepening your understanding. The world of SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront awaits your exploration and transformation!

As you stand at the culmination of this structured learning path, empowered and confident, remember that this series is a gateway—a foundation for continued exploration and growth. Return to these resources and the SAP Learning Site as often as you need, each time expanding your knowledge and reinforcing your expertise.

Happy learning!

entry.title: SAP Solution Managerのセキュリティパッチ適用について
entry.content:
以下のバージョンのSAP Solution ManagerおよびSAP ASEつきまして、
脆弱性を改善するセキュリティパッチがございましたらご教授願います。
※あくまでセキュリティパッチのみだけで、不具合改修および追加機能などのパッチは必要ありません

SAP Solution Manager 7.2
SAP ASE 16.0


English version:

Regarding the following versions of SAP Solution Manager and SAP ASE,
I would like to know if there are any security patches that improve vulnerabilities.
*Please note that this is only a security patch, and no patches for bug fixes or additional features are required.

SAP Solution Manager 7.2
SAP ASE 16.0

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Migration from On-premise S/4HANA to S/4HANA Cloud Pu...
entry.content:
If you want to migrate data from S/4HANA On-prem to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can refer below help links to plan your migration:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/migrating-data-from-s-4-hana-on-premises-to-s-4-hana-cloud/qaq-p/12454492

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Managerのセキュリティパッチ適用について
entry.content:
Solution Managerのあるファンクション「System Recommendations」をご利用いただければ、関連システムに対してのセキュリティノートのリストが表示されます。
リストされたセキュリティノートをお客様の需要に応じて適応していただければよいです。
System Recommendationsの関連情報につきまして、以下のKBAをご参照ください。
2554633 - SysRec: System Recommendations Configuration Guide - SAP Solution Manager 7.2


The "System Recommendations" function in Solution Manager displays a list of security notes for the relevant systems.
You can then adapt the security notes listed according to your needs.
For more information about System Recommendations, see the following KBA:
2554633 - SysRec: System Recommendations Configuration Guide - SAP Solution Manager 7.2

entry.title: How to check if your assets have been created by the SOAP API in UPA?
entry.content:
Introduction

In S/4HANA Private Cloud with Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA), Asset Accounting has published quite a few APIs. Some of them are asynchronous SOAP APIs for users to create and change asset master records. It is because they are asynchronous that right after calling those APIs, you cannot know whether their assets have been created or changed successfully. You have to call the comfirmation services to check the status. (See more details about the APIs from Asset Accounting in UPA in this official help documentation.)

Alternatively, you can check it manually. This short article describes this tool in details. (For more information about Asset Accounting as of S/4HANA 2022, please refer to this article.)

Monitoring Tool

This monitoring tool in the backend has the t-code SRT_MONI. It is especially helpful for customers with backend access. Here are the steps.

  • Start SRT_MONI, and enter your interested time frame in the selection screen
  • You will get a list of all messages occured in the system within that time frame.
  • To get results from Asset Accounting only, filter it on the column 'Sender Interface Namespace', by the service name 'http://sap.com/xi/FI-AA'. (You are looking at the outbound confirmation service. So, AA is the sender.)
  • Now, you get a shorter list.
  • The green and red lights only tell if the system has been configured with a message recevier. They do not really tell us if the APIs have been executed successfully or not. A green light may contain an error from the API, while a red light may show a successful information from the API.
  • Click on the individual message IDs to check out the concrete information.
  • Click on a message ID, you will come to the following screen with concrete errors about why the asset could not be created.

entry.title: Streamlining SAP Background Job Execution with Event-Based Triggers
entry.content:
In some Business Scenarios, we need to collaborate with third-party solutions, such as bank integrations within the system. In such scenarios, scheduling pre-defined standard programs as part of the overall process is often necessary. We encountered a recurring issue with one such program (RFEBKA00), scheduled as a background job in our system to run hourly, checking an Application Server directory for the presence of specific files. Whenever a file was available, the job executed perfectly. However, if no file was present at the time of execution, the job would fail, resulting in unnecessary alerts and false alarms in our monitoring systems. Over time, this not only created confusion but also masked genuine job failures that required immediate attention.

To address this efficiently without deviating from SAP standards, I developed an innovative yet straightforward solution to prevent frequent false alerts and maintain accurate and clean monitoring while still using the original standard program.

There can be other business scenarios as well where this solution can help.

Solution Design:

 

 

 

 

 

 

a) To resolve this, we implemented a wrapper program serving as a pre-check mechanism. This wrapper performs the following actions:

  • Checks the necessary prerequisites (e.g., file presence in an Application Server directory).
  • If prerequisites are met, it triggers the standard SAP job - RFEBKA00 (program - RFEBKA00).
  • If prerequisites are not met, it gracefully exits with an informational message, thus keeping our monitoring precise and relevant.

b) Set up this wrapper program (ZXXX) as background job every hour and change current setup for ‘RFEBKA00’ job to trigger only for event - Z_EVENT’ raised by ZXXX program.

c) If team needs to check what time the file was found, they can still check the background job – ‘RFEBKA00’. Else, if this job was not run, that means, there was no file found at that time.

Technical Steps:

1. Create a new Background Event (Z_EVENT) in Tcode - SM62 (don’t check system event checkbox):

 

 

 

 

2. Create an External Command 'Z_LS' in Tcode - SM49:

 

 

 

 

 

 

3. Create wrapper program – ZXXX with below processing logic:

  • Fetch all the files present in the entered AL11 folder by using FM - 'SXPG_COMMAND_EXECUTE' for below parameters:
    COMMANDNAME = 'Z_LS'
    ADDITIONAL_PARAMETERS = '<AL11 folder path>'
  • If the required file is found in that folder, trigger job - 'RFEBKA00' by using FM – 'BP_EVENT_RAISE' for below parameters:
    EVENTID = 'Z_EVENT'
  • If the event is successfully triggered, display Success message. Else, display information message - 'No file was found for processing in path' and run successfully.

4. Schedule a new background job to run every hour (like it is happening for RFEBKA00 job currently).
Schedule as: Periodic Job (every hour or as required), program Step: ZXXX (wrapper program).

5. Change ‘RFEBKA00’ job scheduling to – ‘only trigger after event – ‘Z_EVENT’, Job frequency: Periodic, keeping Program Steps same.

..And that's it!

Now, the job 'RFEBKA00' will only run if there is a file present in AL11 folder it looks for. This will filter out the case of failed/cancelled jobs because simply the file was not present at the time of execution.

Although there can be different ways to approach this challenge, I believe this solution could be beneficial in other similar business scenarios as well, enhancing operational efficiency and system reliability.

Please let me know your thoughts in the comments.

Cheers! 🎉

entry.title: Terminate Long-Logged-In Users via ABAP Program
entry.content:
How to Automatically Terminate Long-Logged-In Users via ABAP Program

Introduction:

In this blog post, we'll explore how you can implement an ABAP solution to scan user sessions and terminate those that have been active for more than one hour. Although SM04 transaction provides the option to log off the users manually, doing this automatically through ABAP programming adds efficiency.

Step1: To fetch the data from T-Code (SM04) which show the details of user and their systems through submit of standard program (RSM04000_ALV_NEW). CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO=>SET is used to grab ALV output.

Note: If the class-method is not called, u can’t fetch the data through submit program.

Step 2: Retrieve the data using CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO=>GET_DATA_REF and move to internal table as mentioned in the below the image.

Declaration of internal table with limited fields which is used in perform.

Step 3: Get the user log on time from the table(USR02)

Step 4: Loop the data which is fetched from SM04 and read the record of user from USR01. Calculate the system time with logon time. Check the time which is more than an hour and perform the delete_user which can terminate the users who have been logged in more than an hour.

Log off the users through SM04:

Step 1: Go to transaction SM04.

Data of users :

Step 2: Select the user which need to log off and follow the same as image shown.

Step3: If u want to log off the user, click YES.

This is manual process to log off the users.

Conclusion.

By developing a custom ABAP report, we can identify user sessions that have been active for more than one hour and terminate them automatically. To make this solution fully automated, the ABAP report can be scheduled as a background job to run at regular intervals—for example, once every hour. This scheduled job will routinely scan for long-running sessions and take appropriate action without any manual oversight, making it a proactive approach to session management in SAP.

entry.title: Connect SAP S/4HANA Custom CDS Views to Power BI via Odata API
entry.content:
Ever needed a clear, step-by-step guide to seamlessly connect your SAP S/4HANA Custom CDS Views to Power BI? Whether you're looking to enhance your business intelligence, create flexible and interactive dashboards, or visualize data fluctuations effectively through dynamic graphs and charts, this solution is versatile enough for any business scenario. Follow along as we simplify the integration, making your data analysis smoother and more impactful.

In this blog, I’ll walk you through a complete, straightforward process to connect an SAP Custom Core Data Services (CDS) View to Power BI via Odata API. Having struggled myself to find clear, comprehensive steps online, I’ve compiled everything you’ll need right here, saving you the hassle of piecing information together from multiple sources.

This solution was created using SAP Public Cloud version 2502. Please update the variable values in your configuration according to your requirements.

Solution Design

Figure source: SAP Official Document

 

Step-by-Step Guide

(A) SAP S/4HANA Setup

1. Create a Custom CDS View

  • Open your SAP system and navigate to the “Custom CDS Views” app.
  • Click on “Create” and define your CDS view. Ensure the CDS View type is set as “External API.”
  • Save and publish your CDS View. Once published, it will appear in the Communication Scenarios app.

Example CDS view name: YY1_CUSTOM_CDS_VIEW

 

 

 

 

 

 

2. Setup Communication User

  • Go to the “Maintain Communication Users” app.
  • Create a new communication user by clicking on “New.”

Example:

  • Username: TEST_POWERBI_COMM_USER
  • Password: [Set a strong password following SAP guidelines]

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

3. Create Communication Scenario

  • Open “Custom Communication Scenarios” app.
  • Click “New” to create your communication scenario.
  • Add your CDS View to the Inbound and Outbound Services (either or both depending upon the requirement).
    • Inbound Services: Required when you need to send data out of SAP.
    • Outbound Services: Required when you need to receive data into SAP.
  • Save and publish.

Example Communication Scenario: YY1_CUSTOM_POWERBI_SCENARIO

 

 

 

 

 

4. Create Communication System

  • Open “Communication Systems” app.
  • Click “New,” and configure the system, linking it to the communication user you created.
  • Save your configuration.

Example Communication Systems: YY1_CUSTOM_POWERBI_SYSTEMS

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

5. Create Communication Arrangement

  • From the Communication Scenarios app, select your scenario and click “Create Arrangement.”
  • Select the Communication System and Communication Scenario you’ve just created (YY1_CUSTOM_POWERBI_SYSTEMS & YY1_CUSTOM_POWERBI_SCENARIO).
  • Review details and save.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

(B) Power BI Setup

1. Connect to SAP OData Feed

  • Launch Power BI Desktop.
  • Open Power BI app and start with a blank report. Select 'Get data from another source'.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Search for 'Odata Feed' and click 'Connect'.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

2. Authenticate Connection

  • Select 'Basic' and enter the Service URL from Communication Arrangements here:
https://<your-sap-instance>.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/<Your_CDS_View_Name>

 

 

 

 

  • As a one-time activity, select 'Basic' tab, and enter the SAP communication username and password you created earlier leaving the URL same as automatically selected.

 

 

 

 

  • Click “Connect.”

3. Load Data into Power BI

  • Power BI will display the CDS view tables available. Select the required tables and click “Load.”

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

You’re done! Your SAP Custom CDS View data should now be loaded into Power BI for further analysis and visualization.

 

Helpful Tips

  • Always use secure, complex passwords.
  • Regularly check SAP Communication settings to ensure the arrangements are active and secure.

 

References

Thanks to all the resources and their authors those helped me creating this solution 🙂:

  • SAP Official Documentation: External API: Exposing a Custom CDS View as OData API
  • Microsoft Power BI Community
  • LinkedIn post by Joost Flint

Hope this helps streamline your integration process! Feel free to share your comments or experiences below.

Cheers! 🎉

entry.title: Re: Input type number in combination with binding type gi...
entry.content:
Hello robbewuyts,

I understand the problem. It's tricky to get number input right in SAPUI5, especially when you want to handle decimals and different ways people format numbers around the world. Here's the breakdown:

The Problem

Basically, the standard sap.m.Input control, especially when you set it to type="Number", has some limitations with how it handles decimal points (dots and commas) and the way people group numbers (like 1,234.56 or 1.234,56). This becomes even more complex when you want the input to update your data as the user types (valueLiveUdpate="true").

Here's why it's tricky:

  • Browser Behavior: The underlying HTML <input type="number"> element behaves differently across browsers and often defaults to expecting a dot (".") as the decimal separator.

  • Locale Differences: In some countries (like France), a comma (",") is used as the decimal separator, and dots (".") are used for grouping thousands. We need to handle both.

  • Live Updates: When you update the data with every keystroke, you need to validate partial input, which can be complex with these different formats.

The Solution

The most reliable way to handle this is to take control of the validation yourself. Instead of relying on type="Number", we'll use a combination of:

  1. Allowing Flexible Input: We'll let the user type numbers, dots, and commas freely.

  2. Validating as They Type: We'll use the liveChange event to check the input with each keystroke and provide feedback.

  3. Custom Validation Logic: We'll create a function to make sure the number is in a valid format, considering both decimal separators and grouping.

Here's the code snippet:

onLiveChange: function(oEvent) {
    const oSource = oEvent.getSource();
    const sValue = oEvent.getParameter("value");
    const maxDecimalPlaces = 2;

    // 1. Allow only digits, dot, and comma
    if (!this._isValidInput(sValue)) {
        oSource.setValue(sValue.slice(0, -1)); // Remove invalid char
        oSource.setValueState("Error");
        oSource.setValueStateText("Numbers, dot, and comma allowed.");
        return;
    } else {
        oSource.setValueState("None"); // Reset error message
    }

    // 2. Validate the number format (grouping, decimals)
    this._validateNumberFormat(oSource, sValue, maxDecimalPlaces);
},

_isValidInput: function(sValue) {
    const allowedCharsRegex = /^[\d.,]*$/;
    return allowedCharsRegex.test(sValue);
},

_validateNumberFormat: function(oInput, sValue, maxDecimalPlaces) {
    // Handle empty input or just a separator
    if (sValue === "" || sValue === "." || sValue === ",") {
        return;
    }

    // Determine if the decimal is dot or comma
    const decimalSeparator = this._determineDecimalSeparator(sValue);
    const parts = sValue.split(decimalSeparator);
    let integerPart = parts[0];
    let decimalPart = parts[1] || "";

    // Determine grouping separators based on decimal separator
    const groupingSeparatorsRegex = decimalSeparator === "." ? /,/g : /\./g;

    // Validate the integer part (allow grouping separators)
    let cleanedIntegerPart = integerPart.replace(groupingSeparatorsRegex, "");
    if (!/^\d*$/.test(cleanedIntegerPart)) {
        oInput.setValueState("Error");
        oInput.setValueStateText("Invalid number format.");
        return;
    }

    // Validate decimal part length
    if (maxDecimalPlaces !== undefined && maxDecimalPlaces >= 0 && decimalPart.length > maxDecimalPlaces) {
        const truncatedValue = integerPart + decimalSeparator + decimalPart.slice(0, maxDecimalPlaces);
        oInput.setValue(truncatedValue); // Truncate decimals
        oInput.setValueState("Warning");
        oInput.setValueStateText("Max " + maxDecimalPlaces + " decimals.");
        return;
    }

    // Clear warning if it's now valid
    if (oInput.getValueState() === "Warning" && oInput.getValueStateText().includes("decimals")) {
        oInput.setValueState("None");
        oInput.setValueStateText("");
    }
},

_determineDecimalSeparator: function(sValue) {
    const dotIndex = sValue.lastIndexOf(".");
    const commaIndex = sValue.lastIndexOf(",");

    if (dotIndex > commaIndex) {
        return ".";
    } else if (commaIndex > dotIndex) {
        return ",";
    } else {
        return sValue.includes(".") ? "." : sValue.includes(",") ? "," : "."; // Default
    }
},

 

In summary: We're taking control of the input validation to handle different number formats correctly. This gives you the flexibility to support various locales and provide a better user experience. Feel free to modify the code as per your requirement.

I understand it’s a bit complicated, but I hope you find it helpful! I'm also happy to hear any suggestions for improvement, this is the best solution I’ve been able to come up with so far.

Regards,
Farooq

entry.title: Re: Request for Guidance on Material Loan Regularization...
entry.content: @Magdalene_Iremu - Suggest to Create a separate Storage Location for loan if you do not want to consume---> SO System will show you the stock, it would be like storage location to storage location transfer, I believe you know how to transfer that

entry.title: Re: Force mass activation of CDS with dependencies
entry.content:
Excellent solution,
thanks for sharing.

entry.title: Re: "Talent Card Like" report created in Story Report?
entry.content:
Hi,

It would be rather tricky to build it, as it might include too many fields/dimensions and high attentiveness to the details and filters you are putting there. There are no templates or accelerators for that. I would build one page with a card and then filter it every time with an employee name and print/analyze as needed. You need to use Input controls (and Linked analyses for them) + the Text widgets with Dynamic text (separate object for every field/dimension) :

If you manage to build 1 page with 1 talent card, you might copy all the widgets to the same page and regulate them with Input control and linked analysis or duplicate the page several times.

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Core is All You Need | 5. Fine Tuning with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA)
entry.content: Can you please tell how do you did exactly using SAP AI core ? can we fine tune already available OpenAI 's models  in SAP AI core?  If yes , does it need subscription?  ( Consider i m very new to SAP )

entry.title: Azure /IAS User filtering
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I’m using Azure as IdP and IAS as a proxy.
In Azure, I have created groups which are then provisioned to cloud applications via IAS. In IPS I have set up a source system for Azure.

My requirement is to provision only specific groups and their users to IAS – for example, all groups whose names start with "SAP_" – without having to constantly modify the JSON transformation code.

To achieve this, I have configured the following parameters in the source system:

aad.group.filter = startsWith(displayName,'SAP_')

aad.user.filter.group.filter.combine = true

However, it seems that with these settings, all users in Azure are still being queried, which – as far as I understand – can lead to performance issues, especially in large environments.

My questions:

Is there a way to use the aad.user.filter parameter to ensure that only users who belong to the filtered groups (e.g., those with the prefix SAP_) are provisioned, without having to hardcode group names or regularly change the transformation code?

Is it possible to implement something like this:

aad.user.filter = users who belong to the groups defined in aad.group.filter

What is the recommended approach to efficiently and dynamically filter only the required groups and their members?

Thank you in advance for your support

 

Many Thanks

entry.title: Re: RAP - Long Text, larger output and/or Text Editor?
entry.content: @rfreitas87: it would be of greate help if you could please share it?

entry.title: Re: Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers
entry.content:
Hi @mahmoudadel12545 ,

Sorry to hear about the trouble - please reach out to SheerID support here.

entry.title: Re: COGS split into fixed and variable in Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi Achintya, to see Fixed and Variable in market segment is not available today. You need to have a custom report for this. In coming releases, there could be Review Booklet for Market Segments which will show different data points in same report. But for now system show full cost. But when you are looking at market segment, why fixed and variable is required. We need to see gross margin against full COGS. Fixed and variable cost makes sense when you compare costs among different materials and which materials are having more fixed cost portion, to take control actions. But for market segment report it is not much relevance.

entry.title: Re: how to add ID and TEXT of Custom fields in P system.
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, when you create a custom field with a code list (value help), you need to define the values (ID and Text) during development in the Development (Q) system. Here’s how it works, and what you can or cannot do after go-live in the Production (P) system:

Standard Process:

1. Create Custom Field with Code List
App: Custom Fields

    • Use the "Code List" option as the data type.
    • You define the ID and Text values in the development system (Q).

2. Transport to Production

    • After creating the custom field and its values, you must Publish and Include in a transport to send it to the Production system (P).

Can End Users add code list entries directly in P System later?

Unfortunately, no.
In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, code list values are part of the development configuration, so they must be maintained in the Q system and transported to P again. This design ensures consistency and governance in business configuration.

What if customer wants to add new values after Go-Live?

You must:

  1. Go to the Custom Fields app in Q system.
  2. Find the custom field, click on it, and edit the code list values.
  3. Publish the field again.
  4. Include in a new transport request and move it to P system via Export Software Collection app.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Is there any API in sap public cloud to read the full Journal Entry XML data?
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to read existing JE XML data that were created manually by users, in order to customize an external system to send customized JE to SAP Public Cloud version.

However in SAP Business Accelerator Hub portal, I only find APIs that allow me to read JE item data and not the full JE document. These are some API that I tested and only allow me to read JE item data:

API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV
API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV
API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM

Isn't there any API to read a specific JE XML full structure?

I was wondering if I could use other APIs to update existing JE like JOURNALENTRYBULKCHANGEREQUEST and then I could get the full JE XML in the response...

Please help!

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Story reporting - Last Trans Seq
entry.content: "Effective Latest Change" works only for transaction done during 1 day, not useful for a longer period. However, as Jeroen said, you can try to use a filter on "Promotion" event reason + Date range filter (e.g. year, month etc). Hopefully, there won't be multiple promotions per person during the chosen period.

entry.title: Re: Project Settlement in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Christiane,

this is a topic we plan to work on in future. I would be very interested in understanding your detailed requirements. Please get in contact with me (birgit.oettinger@sap.com) if you are interested in an exchange.

Best regards, Birgit

entry.title: Unable to pass Sale Order discount condition value to PO discount condition value
entry.content:
Even after clicking on 'update' in PO conditions tab for updating the custom condition type for discount you are only getting the value from Sales orders condition record and not the changed value in sales order's Respective condition type.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to pass Sale Order discount condition value to...
entry.content:
When you click on Analysis button for the respective condition type in Purchase order, you will see it says  condition record exist and thus its picked  up the value from the same.

So you may have to change the sales order condition record value.
